3 TO 4 FEET OF SWIFT WATER IS FLOWING ACROSS THE ROAD ALONG ABBOTT CREEK ROAD, 2.8 MILES E OF RT.23. THE WATER IS ENTERING INTO THE CRAWL SPACES OF HOMES BELOW CREEKSIDE SUBDIVISION. TRUCK WAS ALSO CAUGHT IN FLOOD WATERS EARLIER TODAY, AND IS NOW FLOATING.

THE CAMPUS OF BETHANY CHRISTIAN SCHOOL IS FLOODED. ONE FOOTBRIDGE IS GONE, ONE IS IN DANGER OF GOING, AND THE BRIDGE THAT CONNECTS THE CAMPUS IS ALSO SUBMERGED. WATER IS GETTING DANGEROUSLY CLOSE TO THE SCHOOL AND ADMIN BUILDING. JUST DOWN THE ROAD, TRAILERS AND HOMES ARE SURROUNDED BY WATER AND IT IS BEGINNING TO CREEP INTO THE HOMES. MANY ROADS ARE ALSO SUBMERGED IN WATER.

*** 1 FATAL *** A ROOF WAS LIFTED OFF A BUILDING AND BLOWN ON THE ROAD BELOW. THIS STRUCK A VEHICLE AND LED TO A FATALITY NEAR THE HOCK SHOP ON W COURT ST IN DOWNTOWN PRESTONBURG.

A HAM RADIO TOWER THAT HAD BEEN MOUNTED ON THE ROOF OF THE ANNEX BUILDING BESIDE THE JAIL, AND BEHIND THE COURTHOUSE IN DOWNTOWN PRESTONSBURG, WAS DESTROYED BY THUNDERSTORM WIND GUSTS. THE WINDS SNAPPED THE SUPPORT CABLES THAT HAD BEEN HOLDING UP THE TOWER, CAUSING THE TOWER TO BREAK IN HALF AND FALL ONTO THE ROOF OF THE BUILDING THAT IT HAD BEEN MOUNTED ON AND THE GROUND NEXT TO THE BUILDING.

A HAM RADIO TOWER THAT HAD BEEN MOUNTED ON THE TOP OF THE ANNEX BUILDING BESIDE THE JAIL AND BEHIND THE COURTHOUSE IN DOWNTOWN PRESTONSBURG WAS DESTROYED BY THUNDERSTORM WIND GUSTS. THE WINDS SNAPPED THE SUPPORT CABLES THAT HAD BEEN HOLDING UP THE TOWER, CAUSING THE TOWER TO BREAK IN HALF AND FALL ONTO THE ROOF OF THE BUILDING IT HAD BEEN MOUNTED ON AND THE GROUND NEXT TO THE BUILDING.

*** 1 FATAL *** UPDATED FOR NWS STORM SURVEY THAT CONFIRMED STRUCTURAL AND TREE DAMAGE IN PRESTONSBURG WAS CAUSED BY DOWNBURST WINDS OF 70 TO 80 MPH. A ROOF WAS LIFTED OFF A BUILDING AND BLOWN ONTO WEST COURT STREET. DEBRIS STRUCK A VEHICLE AND LED TO A FATALITY NEAR THE HOCK SHOP ON W COURT ST IN DOWNTOWN PRESTONBURG. GLASS IN MULTIPLE WINDOWS ON THE NORTH SIDE OF THE BUILDING WAS ALSO BROKEN. ROOF DAMAGE OCCURRED TO OTHER NEARBY BUILDINGS. A FEW TREES WERE ALSO UPROOTED.

TALKED WITH EM IN BRODHEAD AROUND 9 PM. AN OVERVIEW OF THE FLASH FLOODING IMPACTS THAT OCCURRED DURING THE WARNING...4.5 INCHES OF RAIN WAS RECOREDED BETWEEN 4PM AND 9PM, 4 INCHES OF WHICH FELL BEFORE 630 PM. WATER WAS INSIDE 6 BUSINESSES, 1 CHURCH, AND CITY HALL. 2 STORAGE BUILDINGS WERE WASHED OFF FOUNDATION. 6 TO 10 MOBILE HOMES WERE DAMAGED IN SOME CAPACITY. FLOOD WATERS WASHED OUT BLACKTOP ON ONE ROAD, AND AT SOME POINT 7 CITY STREETS, 2 COUNTY ROADS, AND 3 STATE HIGHWAYS WERE UNDER WATER.
